rationale: In the General Discussion, the citing paper uses Dhar and Simonson (2003) as direct evidence that higher choice difficulty increases no-choice/deferral behavior, citing it twice for that mechanism. This is then used to derive an additional implication of the paper's own theory and motivate a supplementary no-choice test. The citation is therefore more than a passing mention, even though it is not part of the paper's core representation-by-comparability hypothesis development.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's difficulty-to-deferral insight to a new theoretical context, arguing that mental representation and option comparability should also shift no-choice rates. It uses the cited work to justify and interpret an auxiliary prediction about choice deferral beyond the main studies.
